Off Calendar Jan-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christopher J. Dyas, Esq. of SACK ROSENDIN (510-286-2200), appearing for defendant / moving party Parsons Infrastructure & Technology Group Inc.
Carole Bosch, Esq. of PAUL & HANLEY (510-559-9980), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Judy L. Devore, individually, and as successor-in-interest to Donald Devore, deceased.

RULING:
Defendant Parsons Infrastructure & Technology Group Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Defendant's objection to plaintiff's late notice of appearance at the hearing was overruled. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff has failed to raise a triable issue whether the systems in question contained asbestos. Plaintiff's discovery responses and deposition testimony were devoid of specific information showing that defendant's employees disturbed asbestos-containing packing or gaskets associated with control valves and/or level transmitters. Plaintiff failed to present sufficient evidence that the system designed, procured and built by Defendant was asbestos-containing or specified asbestos-containing component parts. Kenneth Cohen's declaration does not create a triable issue because that declaration lacks a sufficient foundation for his conclusion regarding asbestos content. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Susan J. Lee, CSR # 4280
